Hi Melanie,

Following our recent discussion, I have put together a revised structure for the Mini Monstar sessions at your center. As you mentioned that some rooms currently have lower attendance, combining these groups will allow you to continue providing the sessions the children love while significantly reducing your overall costs.

For your reference, your current schedule includes two Nursery sessions, one Junior Toddler session, one Toddler session, one Senior Toddler session, and two Kinder sessions, totaling 2.5 hours of session time.

To optimize your budget, I suggest moving to the following streamlined schedule:

- One combined Nursery session (10 minutes total for 11 children)
- One combined Toddler and Junior Toddler session (20 minutes total for 15 children)
- Two Kindy sessions (30 minutes each for up to 22 children per class)

This new arrangement reduces the total time to 1.5 hours, providing a much more cost-effective solution at $260 + GST. Additionally, as we value our partnership, we are happy to provide a further discount to bring the price down to $240 + GST per visit.

Please let me know during the four-session cancellation period if you wish to continue with this new fortnightly schedule starting 22 May. Alternatively, if you prefer not to proceed, the end of this period would mark our final session.

Please let me know if these changes work for your team. Once confirmed, I will update our schedule and provide the adjusted invoice accordingly.

Kind Regards
Katie

patterns.prevention:
• Assign one primary coach per centre with a named backup to eliminate inconsistency and build relationship continuity.

• Audit all centres with multiple coach changes in the past 6 months and implement a 6-week minimum assignment period before rotating staff.

• Create a weekly coverage schedule 2 weeks in advance and communicate any changes to centres by Friday for the following week.

• Schedule manager check-ins with centres showing missed sessions or rescheduled bookings within 5 days to identify coaching or scheduling issues early.

patterns.prevention:
• **Audit** coach engagement and energy levels at all centres within 2 weeks—flag any coaches with attendance issues or feedback about low enthusiasm.

• **Replace** underperforming coaches immediately rather than waiting; assign backup coaches now to centres at risk of losing contracts.

• **Communicate** with centres upfront about coach changes and transition plans so they're not surprised by substitutions or departures.

• **Review** program content and equipment to ensure sessions are age-appropriate, challenging, and varied enough to keep participants engaged.

patterns.prevention:
• Contact centres within first 2-3 sessions to confirm program timing works and gather feedback on coach quality and content relevance.

• Schedule mid-contract check-ins (around session 15-20) to address any emerging concerns before cancellation decisions are made.

• Develop a competitive pricing or value-add proposal for centres considering in-house programs, highlighting cost comparisons and coach expertise benefits.

• Establish a 60-90 day advance notice protocol with centres to allow time for retention conversations and contract adjustments before programs end.

centres.name: Papilio Early Learning Reedy Creek
centres.closedDesc:
Affinity  Group -direction to cut all incursions immediately at center as already over budget. waived 4 session cancellation to assist with confirmation in writing we will be preferred provider as soon as incursions are back email confirmation

Date Started: 28/11/2025
Number of Sessions Completed: 22
Sessions Missed: 0
Rescheduled: 0

Coaches: Mataus ,Patsy

Summary of their time with us: 
The first week of sessions was described as going well, with the coach, Mateu  noted for being polite, professional, and on time, and there were no concerns raised at this early stage. As the program moved into December 2025, feedback remained stable and positive, with no additional bookings requested at that point but still no issues identified, reinforcing that the program had been well received from the outset.

In January 2026, minor operational adjustments were made to better suit the centre’s schedule, including trialling session times between 1:00 pm and 2:30 pm, which were later confirmed as workable. This period reflected a practical alignment phase where the service was being fine-tuned to fit the centre’s routine while maintaining strong satisfaction. By February 2026, direct feedback from the centre continued to be highly positive, with Mateus again described as “great” and “amazing,” consistently on time, and with no complaints raised, indicating growing confidence in the program’s reliability.

In March 2026, further internal coordination took place, including a confirmed adjustment to a 2:00 pm start time, showing continued responsiveness to centre needs. Around the same time, broader feedback highlighted very strong satisfaction, with both coaches Mateus Gomes and Patricia (Patsy) Del Valle being praised for their energy, engagement, and ability to connect with children. Staff noted that the children were actively enjoying the sessions, and the coaches were described as having positive, bubbly personalities and being highly attentive and professional.

By April 2026, the program had fully stabilised and was operating at a consistently high level, with explicit feedback confirming that Mateus was engaging well with children and delivering sessions that were both enjoyable and well-received. Staff reiterated that children loved the activities and that the coach was a strong positive influence in the program, with no concerns raised at this stage.

In May 2026, feedback continued to reflect satisfaction with the program, with the centre confirming they were happy with the sessions and the coach, although Aletia was noted as being away at the time. Shortly after, the centre made the decision to cancel the program due to budget constraints rather than any issues with delivery or satisfaction. The final communication acknowledged that the service had been well liked and that both the coach and sessions had been appreciated, with an indication that they may reconnect in future when budget allows.

Overall, the timeline reflects a consistently positive service experience from start to finish, characterised by strong coach performance, high child engagement, smooth operational adjustments, and stable satisfaction, with the only reason for discontinuation being external financial constraints rather than program quality or delivery issues.

Next point of contact: purely budget happy to have us back asap when they have budget

preCloseStats.tone_reason: Centre is very satisfied with coaches Mateus and Patsy, children are engaged and enjoying sessions.

strong feedback regarding the value of the program and Coach Charlotte's development over time. However, there were several opportunities where we could have strengthened the relationship further. Early in the partnership, feedback received on 20 January 2026 indicated that sessions were initially "rocky," with concerns around coach engagement, one-on-one interaction with children, and communication with educators. The positive relationship was maintained throughout the cancellation process, and the centre specifically invited future discussions in the new financial year. Going forward, earlier coach support, stronger educator-level engagement, streamlined invoicing assistance, and more consistent communication pathways could further strengthen similar centre relationships and improve retention opportunities where external budget pressures arise.
